data.content:
Implementation plan

Summary
- Primary bug: selecting "All Repositories" is immediately undone by the RepoSwitcher registry-sync effect, which re-applies a persisted/default repo whenever activeRepo is null (src/components/repo-switcher.tsx lines 34-44). This explains the reported behavior across screens because the global repo selection never stays on the all-repos state.
- Secondary gap: creation is not fully forced through repo choice in all-repos mode. The header already renders a repo dropdown when !activeRepo && registeredRepos.length > 1 (src/components/app-header.tsx lines 290-311), but the Shift+N shortcut still opens the create dialog directly against defaultRepo (src/components/app-header.tsx lines 83-105), bypassing repo selection.
- Existing aggregation paths already exist for the major screens once the global selection can remain null: beats list (src/app/beats/page.tsx lines 129-174), human action (src/components/final-cut-view.tsx lines 17-50), retakes (src/components/retakes-view.tsx lines 485-543), and history via the server-side all-log scan when no _repo is passed (src/components/agent-history-view.tsx lines 571-580, src/lib/agent-history.ts lines 561-677).

Planned changes
- Update src/components/repo-switcher.tsx so registry hydration only restores/defaults a repo on true initial bootstrap, not whenever activeRepo becomes null later. The effect should preserve an intentional all-repos selection and only auto-pick a repo when the app starts without an explicit URL repo and without an intentional all-repos choice.
- Likely use a one-time guard tied to initial hydration or explicit URL presence rather than the current if (!activeRepo && data.data.length > 0) branch. Preserve current behavior for first load/persisted repo restoration, but do not call updateUrl({ repo: ... }) after the user has actively selected all repos.
- Update src/components/app-header.tsx so Shift+N respects shouldChooseRepo. In all-repos mode with multiple repos, the shortcut should open the same repo-selection affordance or chooser instead of silently selecting defaultRepo.
- Review the action-button copy and behavior in all-repos mode. The current UI already uses a dropdown button, but the label is New; confirm whether it should be renamed to Add or otherwise aligned with the request while still forcing project choice.
- Verify CreateBeatDialog can continue to receive an explicit repo path only; no dialog-level multi-repo create mode should be introduced (src/components/create-beat-dialog.tsx lines 33-188).

Test strategy
- Add or update a characterization test around RepoSwitcher or URL-state behavior to prove selecting all repos is sticky and not overwritten by the registry sync effect.
- Add or update a header interaction test or source-level contract test covering the all-repos create path so keyboard-triggered creation cannot default into an arbitrary repo when multiple repos are registered.
- Run focused tests for repo selection, URL sync, and create-button behavior first, then full quality gates for the implementation step: bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, bun run build.

Complexity and risks
- Low-to-moderate implementation scope; the main risk is regressing initial repo restoration for first-time loads or deep links.
- Keyboard behavior is a second risk area because there is currently no visible chooser state separate from the dropdown menu button, so implementation may require introducing a small dedicated chooser state or reusing an existing dropdown trigger pattern.
- History, final-cut, and retakes likely need no data-layer changes unless testing reveals a separate screen-specific assumption about activeRepo being non-null.

Child knots
- No child knots are necessary at planning time; this is a contained fix spanning repo selection state, header create behavior, and associated tests.
